steps.title: Write the two angle types
steps.content:
Two intersecting lines make two equal vertical angles and two equal adjacent angles. If one angle is $(8x-130)^\circ$, then the opposite angle has the same measure. Each adjacent angle is supplementary:
$$180-(8x-130)=310-8x$$

steps.title: List possible two-angle sums
steps.content:
The only distinct sums come from two given-type angles, two adjacent-type angles, or one of each:
$$(8x-130)+(8x-130)=16x-260$$
$$(310-8x)+(310-8x)=-16x+620$$
$$(8x-130)+(310-8x)=180$$

steps.title: Find the impossible sum
steps.content:
The possible sums are $(16x-260)^\circ$, $(-16x+620)^\circ$, and $180^\circ$. The expression $(-16x+260)^\circ$ is not equivalent to any of them, so it could not be the sum of any two angles.
The answer is A.
